segments.text: David organized the men who were with him and put commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds in charge of them.

David sent the army out divided into three sections. One third was commanded by Joab, one third was commanded by Abishai, son of Zeruiah, Joab's brother, and one third was commanded by Ittai the Gittite. The king told the men,
I myself will go out to battle with you.
segments.speaker: One third was

segments.text: But the men replied,

No, you must not go out into battle! For if we have to run away, they won't care about us. Even if half of us die, they won't care about that either. But you are worth ten thousand of us, so it's better if you stay here and send us help from the town.
segments.speaker: One third was

segments.text: I will do whatever you think best,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king replied. The king stood beside the gate while all his men marched out by hundreds and by thousands.
The king ordered Joab, Abishai and Ittai,
Treat young Absalom gently for me.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: All the men heard the king giving orders to each of his commanders about Absalom.
David's army marched out to face the Israelites in battle, which was fought in the forest of Ephraim.
The Israelites were defeated by David's men and many were killed that day—some twenty thousand.
The battle covered the whole countryside, and that day more died because of the forest than were killed by the sword.

Absalom ran into some of David's men while he was riding on his mule. As the mule went under the twisted branches of a large oak tree, Absalom's hair got caught in the tree. The mule he was riding kept going, leaving him hanging between earth and sky.
One of David's men saw what happened, so he told Joab,
I just saw Absalom hanging from an oak tree!
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: What! You saw him like that?
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Joab said to the man.
Why didn't you kill him right then and there? I would have given you ten shekels of silver and a soldier's belt as a reward!
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: But the man replied,
Even if you gave me a thousand shekels of silver, I wouldn't hurt the king's son. We all heard the king give the order to you, Abishai, and Ittai, ‘Look after young Absalom for me.’
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: If I had disobeyed and killed Absalom—and the king finds out everything—you yourself wouldn't have defended me.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: I'm not going to waste time waiting around like this with you!
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Joab told him. He grabbed three spears and drove them into Absalom's heart while he was still alive, hanging from the oak tree.
Ten of Joab's armor-bearers surrounded Absalom and hacked him to death.
Then Joab blew the ram's horn, and his men stopped chasing the Israelites because Joab had signaled them to stop.
They took Absalom and threw him into a deep pit in the forest, and piled a large heap of stones over him. All the Israelites ran away to their homes.
Absalom while he was alive had made a stone pillar and set it up in the King's Valley as a memorial to himself, for he thought to himself,
I don't have a son to keep the memory of my name alive.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He named the pillar after himself, and it's called Absalom's Monument even today.
Then Ahimaaz, son of Zadok, said,
Please let me run and take the good news to the king that the Lord has vindicated him over his enemies.
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: You're not the man to take the good news today,
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Joab replied.
You can do it some other time, but don't do it today, because the king's son is dead.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: So Joab said to a man from Ethiopia,
Go and tell the king what you have seen.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: He bowed to Joab and ran off.
Ahimaaz asked Joab again,
Never mind what happens, please let me run too, after the Ethiopian!
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: Son, why do you want to run? You won't get anything for it.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Joab replied.
Doesn't matter, I want to run anyway,
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: he said.
Fine, start running!
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Joab told him. Ahimaaz took the route over flatter ground and overtook the Ethiopian.
David was sitting between the inside and outside gates. The watchman climbed up to the roof of the gateway by the wall. He looked out, and saw a man running by himself.
So he shouted down to tell the king.
If he's by himself then he's bringing good news,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king replied. As the first runner got closer,
the watchman saw someone else running, and he shouted down to the gatekeeper,
Look! There's another man running by himself!
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: He'll also be bringing good news,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: said the king.
The first man seems to me to be running like Ahimaaz, son of Zadok,
segments.speaker: The watchman

segments.text: said the watchman.
He's a good man,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king replied.
He'll bring good news.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: Ahimaaz shouted out greetings to the king. Then he came and he bowed facedown before the king.
Blessed be the Lord your God!
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: he said.
He has defeated the men who rebelled against Your Majesty!
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: How is young Absalom? Is he all right?
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king asked. Ahimaaz answered,
It was very chaotic when your officer Joab sent me, your servant. I really don't know what was happening.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: St and to one side and wait,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king told him. So Ahimaaz stood to one side and waited.
Right then the Ethiopian arrived and said,
Your Majesty, listen to the good news! Today the Lord has defeated all those who rebelled against you!
segments.speaker: Right then the Ethiopian arrived and

segments.text: How is young Absalom? Is he all right?
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king asked. The Ethiopian replied,
May what has happened to the young man happen to Your Majesty's enemies, and to everyone who rebels against you!
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: The king broke down. He went up to the room over the gate and cried. As he walked, he sobbed out,
My son Absalom! My son, my son Absalom! I wish I'd died instead of you, Absalom, my son, my son!
segments.speaker: The king
